role_title: Senior Product Manager - Billing and Pricing

location.office_time_required: In-person environment: Union Square office designed for ambitious builders

level_signals: Described as 'An experienced, senior Product Manager'
Owns strategy, roadmap, and execution end to end — solo ownership signal
Partners with finance, GTM, and leadership — cross-functional senior scope

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: An experienced, senior Product Manager with a strong background building and owning pricing or monetization systems at high-growth B2B SaaS companies.
Hands-on experience with usage- or consumption-based pricing, including the billing, metering, and infrastructure that make it work.
Strong commercial and product judgment, with the ability to balance customer value, business outcomes, and long-term incentive alignment.
Comfort working across the stack, from polished user-facing experiences to backend systems, and partnering tightly with engineering and finance.
Fluency with data and experimentation, and the rigor to make pricing decisions defensible.
High standards, low ego, and a bias toward ownership and outcomes.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Genuine excitement about reinventing how software is monetized in an AI-native world.

scope.surface: Consumption-based pricing system — in-product billing experience and backend billing/metering infrastructure; product pricing decisions across models and features
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, consumption-based (token/usage) pricing, legal AI
primary_metrics_inferred: ARR growth driven by pricing strategy
core_responsibilities: Own the strategy, roadmap, and execution for our consumption-based pricing system, from the in-product experience to the underlying billing and metering infrastructure.
Build and operate the systems that reliably turn usage (tokens) into revenue (dollars), partnering closely with engineering on accuracy, transparency, and trust.
Own product pricing decisions: how and when we price newly released models, how we price features, and what is included, bundled, add-on, or metered.
Continuously evolve our monetization strategy to keep our incentives aligned with our customers' and their end clients'.
Partner with go-to-market, finance, and leadership to translate pricing strategy into packaging, billing, and forecasting that scale.
Use data and customer insight to run pricing experiments, measure their impact, and turn results into a prioritized roadmap.
Deeply understand how legal teams buy and consume software, and translate that into pricing that is clear, fair, and durable.

green_flags: Clear and specific mandate — consumption-based pricing is a well-scoped, consequential surface area
Company at meaningful scale ($100M+ ARR) with room to grow — impact is real
First major legal AI company to move to consumption-based pricing — cutting-edge strategic context
Comprehensive benefits package explicitly listed (medical, dental, vision, parental leave, 401K with match, unlimited PTO)
Company is growing through acquisitions — signals financial health and ambition
red_flags: In-person only at Union Square (NYC) — fully eliminates remote candidates; no flexibility mentioned
Visa/work authorization not addressed at all
No years-of-experience floor stated explicitly — seniority bar is vague beyond 'experienced, senior'

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title and JD language; no comp range provided)
location.cities_listed (inferred from 'Union Square office' reference — Union Square is in NYC/Manhattan)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and pricing context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no mention of equity anywhere in JD)
ambiguities: Work authorization and visa sponsorship are entirely unaddressed in the JD — marked as 'unclear'
No explicit years-of-experience number given; 'experienced, senior' is the only signal — classified as hard based on unconditional bullet language
